title: Creamy Argentinian Beef Stir-fry
description: A rich and creamy dish featuring ground cumin, perfect for showing off in a dinner setting. Sugar-free-friendly.
instructions: Slice the beef into thin strips and set aside.
Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
In a large skillet or wok, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onions to the skillet and sauté until translucent.
Fold in minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Season with ground cumin and red pepper flakes if using. Add a pinch of sugar (for those who prefer a little sweetness) to taste, then toss everything together thoroughly.
Drain the tomato sauce through a sieve or cheesecloth to remove excess liquid, then add it to the skillet along with 100ml of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil and let simmer for about 5 minutes until slightly thickened.
Pour in the heavy cream and stir well. Cook for another 3-4 minutes or until the sauce reaches desired consistency.
Add the beef strips, reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for an additional 10 minutes until the beef is fully cooked.
Remove from heat and serve hot over rice or as a side dish.
